info.title: TzKT API
info.description:
# Introduction

TzKT is the most widely used tool in Tezos that provides you with convenient and flexible access to the Tezos blockchain data, processed and indexed by its own indexer. 
You can fetch all historical data via REST API, or subscribe for real-time data via WebSocket API. TzKT was built by the joint efforts of the entire Tezos community 
to help developers build more services and dapps on top of Tezos.

TzKT Indexer and API are [open-source](https://github.com/baking-bad/tzkt), so don't be afraid to depend on the third-party service,
because you can always clone, build and run it yourself to have full control over all the components.

Feel free to contact us if you have any questions or feature requests.
Your feedback is much appreciated!

- Discord: https://discord.gg/aG8XKuwsQd
- Telegram: https://t.me/baking_bad_chat
- X: https://x.com/TezosBakingBad
- Email: hello@bakingbad.dev

And don't forget to star TzKT [on GitHub](https://github.com/baking-bad/tzkt) if you like it 😊

# Get Started

There are two API services provided for public use:
- **Free TzKT API** with free anonymous access;
- **TzKT Pro** with paid subscriptions with increased rate limits, off-chain data, extended support and business-level SLA.

You can find more details about differences between available tiers [here](https://tzkt.io/api).

## Free TzKT API

Free-tier TzKT API is the best way to get started and explore available Tezos data and API functionality.
It doesn't require authorization and is free for everyone and for both commercial and non-commercial use.

> #### Note: attribution required
If you use free-tier TzKT API, you **must** mention it on your website or application by placing the label
"Powered by TzKT API", or "Built with TzKT API", or "Data provided by TzKT API" with a direct link to [tzkt.io](https://tzkt.io).

It's available for the following Tezos networks with the following base URLs:

- Mainnet: `https://api.tzkt.io/` or `https://api.mainnet.tzkt.io/` ([view docs](https://api.tzkt.io))
- Ghostnet: `https://api.ghostnet.tzkt.io/` ([view docs](https://api.ghostnet.tzkt.io))
- Shadownet: `https://api.shadownet.tzkt.io/` ([view docs](https://api.shadownet.tzkt.io))
- Seoulnet: `https://api.seoulnet.tzkt.io/` ([view docs](https://api.seoulnet.tzkt.io))
- Tallinnnet: `https://api.tallinnnet.tzkt.io/` ([view docs](https://api.tallinnnet.tzkt.io))

### Sending Requests

To send a request to Free TzKT API you need literally nothing. Just take the base URL of the particular network
(for example, Tezos mainnet: `https://api.tzkt.io`) and append the path of the particular endpoint
(for example, chain's head: `/v1/head`), that's pretty much it: 

```bash
curl https://api.tzkt.io/v1/head
```

Read through this documentation to explore available endpoints, query parameters
(note, if you click on a query parameter, you will see available modes, such as `.eq`, `.in`, etc.)
and response models. If you have any questions, do not hesitate to ask for support, Tezos community has always been very friendly! 😉

### Rate Limits

Please, refer to https://tzkt.io/api to check relevant rate limits.

If you exceed the limit, the API will respond with `HTTP 429` status code.

## TzKT Pro

TzKT Pro is intended for professional use, for those who seek for extended capabilities, performance, reliability and business-level SLA.
TzKT Pro service is provided via paid subscriptions. Please, refer to [Pricing Plans](https://tzkt.io/api) to check available tiers.

It's available for the following Tezos networks with the following base URLs:

- Mainnet: `https://pro.tzkt.io/` ([view docs](https://api.tzkt.io))
- Testnets: *let us know if you need TzKT Pro for testnets*

### Authorization

To access TzKT Pro you will need to authorize requests with your personal API key, that you will receive on your email after purchasing a subscription.
This can be done by adding the query string parameter `?apikey={your_key}` or by adding the HTTP header `apikey: {your_key}`.

Note that you can have multiple API keys within a single subscription.

Keep your API keys private, do not publish it anywhere and do not hardcode it, especially in public repositories.
If your key was compromised, just let us know and we will issue a new one.

Also note that passing the API key via HTTP headers is more secure, because in HTTPS headers are encrypted,
but query string is not, so the key can be unintentionally exposed to third parties.

### Sending Requests

Sending a request with the API key passed as a query string parameter:

```bash
curl https://pro.tzkt.io/v1/head?apikey={your_key}
```

Sending a request with the API key passed via an HTTP header:

```bash
curl https://pro.tzkt.io/v1/head \
    -H 'apikey: {your_key}'
```

### Rate Limits

Please, refer to https://tzkt.io/api to check relevant rate limits for different pricing plans.

Also, TzKT Pro provides you with the additional HTTP headers to show the allowed limits, number of available requests
and the time remaining (in seconds) until the quota is reset. Here's an example:

```
RateLimit-Limit: 50
RateLimit-Remaining: 49
RateLimit-Reset: 1
```

It also sends general information about your rate limits per second and per day:

```
X-RateLimit-Limit-Second: 50
X-RateLimit-Remaining-Second: 49
X-RateLimit-Limit-Day: 3000000
X-RateLimit-Remaining-Day: 2994953
```

If you exceed the limit, the API will respond with `HTTP 429` status code.

get.description: Returns a list of accounts.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filters by internal id.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Filters by address.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filters accounts by type (`user`, `delegate`, `contract`, `rollup`, `smart_rollup`, `ghost`).

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Filters accounts by contract kind (`delegator_contract` or `smart_contract`)

parameters.name: delegate
parameters.description: Filters accounts by delegate. Allowed fields for `.eqx` mode: none.

parameters.name: stakedPseudotokens
parameters.description: Filters accounts by amount of staked pseudotokens.

parameters.name: balance
parameters.description: Filters accounts by balance

parameters.name: staked
parameters.description: Filters accounts by participation in staking

parameters.name: lastActivity
parameters.description: Filters accounts by last activity level (where the account was updated)

parameters.name: select
parameters.description: Specify comma-separated list of fields to include into response or leave it undefined to return full object. If you select single field, response will be an array of values in both `.fields` and `.values` modes.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sorts delegators by specified field. Supported fields: `id` (default), `balance`, `rollupBonds`, `firstActivity`, `lastActivity`, `numTransactions`, `numContracts`.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Specifies which or how many items should be skipped

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

parameters.name: addresses
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of account addresses to get activity of.

parameters.name: roles
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of activity roles (`sender`, `target`, `initiator`, `mention`) to filter activity by.

parameters.name: types
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of activity types (`activation`, `autostaking`, `baking`, `ballot`, `dal_attestation_reward`,
            `dal_entrapment_evidence`, `dal_publish_commitment`, `delegation`, `double_baking`, `double_consensus`, `drain_delegate`, 
            `attestation`, `attestation_reward`, `increase_paid_storage`, `migration`, `nonce_revelation`, `origination`, `preattestation`, `proposal`,
            `register_constant`, `reveal`, `revelation_penalty`, `set_delegate_parameters`, `set_deposits_limit`, `sr_add_messages`, `sr_cement`, `sr_execute`,
            `sr_originate`, `sr_publish`, `sr_recover_bond`, `sr_refute`, `staking`, `transaction`, `transfer_ticket`, `tx_rollup_commit`, `tx_rollup_dispatch_tickets`,
            `tx_rollup_finalize_commitment`, `tx_rollup_origination`, `tx_rollup_rejection`, `tx_rollup_remove_commitment`, `tx_rollup_return_bond`, `tx_rollup_submit_batch`,
            `vdf_revelation`, `update_secondary_key`, `ticket_transfer`, `token_transfer`)

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Filter activity by timestamp.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort mode: 0 - asc (oldest to newest), 1 - desc (newest to oldest).

parameters.name: lastId
parameters.description: Id of the last activity element received, which is used for cursor pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of elements to return.

parameters.name: micheline
parameters.description: Format of the parameters, storage and diffs: `0` - JSON, `1` - JSON string, `2` - raw micheline, `3` - raw micheline string.

parameters.name: quote
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of ticker symbols to inject historical prices into response.

get.description: Returns account balance

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Account address (starting with tz or KT)

get.description:
Returns account balance* at the specified block.  
\* - for non-baker tz-accounts historical balances do not include staked tez,
because stakers do not really have staked tez on their balance, they have staking pseudotokens instead.
If you want to get a full historical balance, including staked tez, use the Tezos node RPC:
`/chains/main/blocks/{level}/context/contracts/{address}/full_balance`.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Account address (starting with tz or KT)

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Block height at which you want to know account balance

get.description:
Returns account balance* at the specified datetime.  
\* - for non-baker tz-accounts historical balances do not include staked tez,
because stakers do not really have staked tez on their balance, they have staking pseudotokens instead.
If you want to get a full historical balance, including staked tez, use the Tezos node RPC:
`/chains/main/blocks/{level}/context/contracts/{address}/full_balance`.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Account address (starting with tz or KT)

parameters.name: datetime
parameters.description: Datetime at which you want to know account balance (e.g. `2020-01-01`, or `2019-12-30T23:42:59Z`)

get.description:
Returns time series with historical balances* (only changes, without duplicates).  
\* - for non-baker tz-accounts historical balances do not include staked tez,
because stakers do not really have staked tez on their balance, they have staking pseudotokens instead.
If you want to get a full historical balance, including staked tez, use the Tezos node RPC:
`/chains/main/blocks/{level}/context/contracts/{address}/full_balance`.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Account address (starting with tz or KT)

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step of the time series, for example if `step = 1000` you will get balances at blocks `1000, 2000, 3000, ...`.

parameters.name: select
parameters.description: Specify comma-separated list of fields to include into response or leave it undefined to return full object. If you select single field, response will be an array of values in both `.fields` and `.values` modes.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sorts historical balances by specified field. Supported fields: `level`.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Specifies which or how many items should be skipped

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

parameters.name: quote
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of ticker symbols to inject historical prices into response

get.description: Exports account balance report in .csv format

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Account address (starting with tz or KT)

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the datetime range to filter by (ISO 8601, e.g. 2019-11-31)

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the datetime range to filter by (ISO 8601, e.g. 2019-12-31)

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency to convert amounts to (`btc`, `eur`, `usd`, `cny`, `jpy`, `krw`, `eth`, `gbp`)

parameters.name: historical
parameters.description: `true` if you want to use historical prices, `false` to use current price

parameters.name: delimiter
parameters.description: Column delimiter (`comma`, `semicolon`)

parameters.name: separator
parameters.description: Decimal separator (`comma`, `point`)

Returns a list of bigmaps.

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description: Filters bigmaps by smart contract address.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Filters bigmaps by path in the contract storage.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Filters bigmaps by tags: `metadata`, `token_metadata`, `ledger`.

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: Filters bigmaps by status: `true` - active, `false` - removed.

parameters.name: lastLevel
parameters.description: Filters bigmaps by the last update level.

parameters.name: select
parameters.description: Specify comma-separated list of fields to include into response or leave it undefined to return full object. If you select single field, response will be an array of values in both `.fields` and `.values` modes.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sorts bigmaps by specified field. Supported fields: `id` (default), `ptr`, `firstLevel`, `lastLevel`, `totalKeys`, `activeKeys`, `updates`.

parameters.name: offset

get.description: Returns a list of all bigmap keys.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
Filter by internal TzKT id.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: bigmap
parameters.description:
Filter by bigmap ptr.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: Filters by status: `true` - active, `false` - removed.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description:
Filter by key hash.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description:
Filter by key.  
Note, this parameter supports the following format: `key{.path?}{.mode?}=...`,
so you can specify a path to a particular field to filter by (for example, `?key.foo.in=bar,baz`).  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description:
Filter by value.  
Note, this parameter supports the following format: `value{.path?}{.mode?}=...`,
so you can specify a path to a particular field to filter by (for example, `?value.foo.in=bar,baz`).  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: firstLevel
parameters.description:
Filter by level of the block where the key was first seen.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: firstTime
parameters.description:
Filter by timestamp (ISO 8601) of the block where the key was first seen.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: lastLevel
parameters.description:
Filter by level of the block where the key was last seen.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: lastTime
parameters.description:
Filter by timestamp (ISO 8601) of the block where the key was last seen.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: updates
parameters.description:
Filter by number of actions with the bigmap key.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Sorts items (asc or desc) by the specified field.
You can see what fields can be used for sorting in the response description, below.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
Specifies which or how many items should be skipped.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return.

parameters.name: select
parameters.description:
Specify a comma-separated list of fields to include into response or leave it undefined to get default set of fields.
This parameter accepts values of the following format: `{field}{path?}{as alias?}`, so you can do deep selection
(for example, `?select=balance,token.metadata.symbol as token,...`).  
Note, if you select just one field, the response will be flatten into a simple array of values.  
Click on the parameter to expand the details.

parameters.name: micheline
parameters.description: Format of the `key` and `value` fields: `0` - JSON, `2` - Micheline

get.description: Returns a list of origination operations.

parameters.name: anyof
parameters.description: Filters originations by any of the specified fields. Example: `anyof.sender.initiator=tz1...` will return operations where `sender` OR `initiator` is equal to the specified value. This parameter is useful when you need to retrieve all originations associated with a specified account.

parameters.name: initiator
parameters.description: Filters origination operations by initiator. Allowed fields for `.eqx` mode: `contractDelegate`.

parameters.name: sender
parameters.description: Filters origination operations by sender. Allowed fields for `.eqx` mode: `contractDelegate`.

parameters.name: contractDelegate
parameters.description: Filters origination operations by delegate. Allowed fields for `.eqx` mode: `initiator`, `sender`.

parameters.name: originatedContract
parameters.description: Filters origination operations by originated contract. Allowed fields for `.eqx` mode: none.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filters origination operations by internal TzKT id

parameters.name: typeHash
parameters.description: Filters origination operations by 32-bit hash of originated contract parameter and storage types (helpful for searching originations of similar contracts)

parameters.name: codeHash
parameters.description: Filters origination operations by 32-bit hash of originated contract code (helpful for searching originations of same contracts)

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Filters origination operations by level.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Filters origination operations by timestamp.

parameters.name: senderCodeHash
parameters.description: Filters by `senderCodeHash`.

parameters.name: anyCodeHash
parameters.description: Filters by either `senderCodeHash` or `codeHash`.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filters origination operations by operation status (`applied`, `failed`, `backtracked`, `skipped`).

parameters.name: select
parameters.description: Specify comma-separated list of fields to include into response or leave it undefined to return full object. If you select single field, response will be an array of values in both `.fields` and `.values` modes.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sorts originations by specified field. Supported fields: `id` (default), `level`, `gasUsed`, `storageUsed`, `bakerFee`, `storageFee`, `allocationFee`, `contractBalance`.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Specifies which or how many items should be skipped

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

parameters.name: micheline
parameters.description: Format of the parameters, storage and diffs: `0` - JSON, `1` - JSON string, `2` - raw micheline, `3` - raw micheline string

parameters.name: quote
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of ticker symbols to inject historical prices into response

This parameter is useful when you need to retrieve all transactions associated with a specified account.

parameters.name: initiator
parameters.description: Filters transactions by initiator. Allowed fields for `.eqx` mode: `target`.

parameters.name: sender

parameters.name: target

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Filters transactions by amount (micro tez).

parameters.description: Filters by `targetCodeHash`.

parameters.name: codeHash
parameters.description: Filters by either `senderCodeHash` or `targetCodeHash`.

parameters.name: entrypoint
parameters.description: Filters transactions by entrypoint called on the target contract.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description:
Filters transactions by parameter value. Note, this query parameter supports the following format: `?parameter{.path?}{.mode?}=...`,
            so you can specify a path to a particular field to filter by, for example: `?parameter.token_id=...` or `?parameter.sigs.0.ne=...`.
            Also, note that `.value` part must be omitted in the path, so, for example, filtering by `parameter.value.foo` must be specified as `?parameter.foo=...`.

parameters.name: hasInternals
parameters.description: Filters transactions by presence of internal operations.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filters transactions by operation status (`applied`, `failed`, `backtracked`, `skipped`).

parameters.name: select
parameters.description: Specify comma-separated list of fields to include into response or leave it undefined to return full object. If you select single field, response will be an array of values in both `.fields` and `.values` modes.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sorts transactions by specified field. Supported fields: `id` (default), `level`, `gasUsed`, `storageUsed`, `bakerFee`, `storageFee`, `allocationFee`, `amount`.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Specifies which or how many items should be skipped

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

parameters.name: micheline
parameters.description: Format of the parameters, storage and diffs: `0` - JSON, `1` - JSON string, `2` - raw micheline, `3` - raw micheline string

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of ticker symbols to inject historical prices into response

parameters.description:

Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.description:
Filter by contract address.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: tokenId
parameters.description:
Filter by tokenId (for FA1.2 tokens tokenId is always `"0"`).  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: globalId
parameters.description:
Filter by "global id", which is a `"{contract}:{tokenId}"` string.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: standard
parameters.description:
Filter by token standard (`fa1.2` or `fa2`).  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: totalMinted
parameters.description:
Filter by total minted.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: totalBurned
parameters.description:
Filter by total burned. Note: total burned shows only those tokens that were literally burned,
excluding tokens sent to burn addresses (because they still exist on the burn address' balance).  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: totalSupply
parameters.description:
Filter by total supply. Note: total supply shows all existing tokens,
including tokens sent to burn addresses (so that they exist on the burn address' balance).  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: firstMinter
parameters.description:
Filter by address of the first minter.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.description:
Filter by level of the block where the token was first seen.  
Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: firstTime
parameters.description:

Click on the parameter to expand more details.

parameters.name: lastLevel

type.description:
Type of the account (`user` - simple wallet account, `delegate` - account, registered as a delegate (baker),
`contract` - smart contract programmable account, `ghost` - non-existent contract, `empty` - account hasn't appeared in the blockchain yet,
`rollup` - transactional optimistic rollup, `smart_rollup` - smart contract optimistic rollup)

id.description: Internal TzKT id

address.description: Public key hash of the account

alias.description: Name of the project behind the account or account description

publicKey.description: Base58 representation of account's public key, revealed by the account

revealed.description: Public key revelation status. Unrevealed account can't send manager operation (transaction, origination etc.)

rollupBonds.description: Amount of tx rollup commitment bonds (micro tez)

smartRollupBonds.description: Amount of smart rollup commitment bonds (micro tez)

stakedBalance.description:
Amount staked with the selected baker (micro tez).
Like delegated amount, except for it is frozen and can be slashed.

stakedPseudotokens.description: Amount of "pseudo-tokens" received after staking. These pseudotokens are used for unstaking.

unstakedBalance.description: Amount that was unstaked, but not yet finalized (i.e. it is still frozen) (micro tez)

unstakedBaker.description: Information about the baker, for which there are pending unstake requests

counter.description: An account nonce which is used to prevent operation replay

delegate.description: Information about the current delegate of the account. `null` if it's not delegated

delegationLevel.description: Block height of latest delegation. `null` if it's not delegated

delegationTime.description: Block datetime of latest delegation (ISO 8601, e.g. `2020-02-20T02:40:57Z`). `null` if it's not delegated

numContracts.description: Number of contracts, created (originated) and/or managed by the account

rollupsCount.description: Number of tx rollups, created (originated) by the account

smartRollupsCount.description: Number of smart rollups, created (originated) by the account

activeTokensCount.description: Number of account tokens with non-zero balances

tokenBalancesCount.description: Number of tokens the account ever had

tokenTransfersCount.description: Number of token transfers from/to the account

activeTicketsCount.description: Number of tickets the account owns.

ticketBalancesCount.description: Number of tickets the account ever owned.

ticketTransfersCount.description: Number of ticket transfers from/to the account.

numActivations.description:
Number of account activation operations. Are used to activate accounts that were recommended allocations of
tezos tokens for donations to the Tezos Foundation’s fundraiser

numDelegations.description: Number of delegation operations, related to the account

numOriginations.description: Number of all origination (deployment / contract creation) operations, related to the account

numTransactions.description: Number of all transaction (tez transfer) operations, related to the account

numReveals.description: Number of reveal (is used to reveal the public key associated with an account) operations of the contract

numRegisterConstants.description: Number of register global constant operations sent by the account

numSetDepositsLimits.description: Number of set deposits limit operations sent by the account

numMigrations.description:
Number of migration (result of the context (database) migration during a protocol update) operations,
related to the account (synthetic type)

txRollupOriginationCount.description: Number of tx rollup origination operations sent by the account

txRollupSubmitBatchCount.description: Number of tx rollup submit batch operations sent by the account

txRollupCommitCount.description: Number of tx rollup commit operations sent by the account

txRollupReturnBondCount.description: Number of tx rollup return bond operations sent by the account

txRollupFinalizeCommitmentCount.description: Number of tx rollup finalize commitment operations sent by the account

txRollupRemoveCommitmentCount.description: Number of tx rollup remove commitment operations sent by the account

txRollupRejectionCount.description: Number of tx rollup rejection operations sent by the account

txRollupDispatchTicketsCount.description: Number of tx rollup dispatch tickets operations sent by the account

transferTicketCount.description: Number of transfer ticket operations sent by the account

increasePaidStorageCount.description: Number of `increase_paid_storage` operations sent by the account

drainDelegateCount.description: Number of `drain_delegate` operations related to the account

smartRollupAddMessagesCount.description: Number of `smart_rollup_add_messages` operations related to the account

smartRollupCementCount.description: Number of `smart_rollup_cement` operations related to the account

smartRollupExecuteCount.description: Number of `smart_rollup_execute_outbox_message` operations related to the account

smartRollupOriginateCount.description: Number of `smart_rollup_originate` operations related to the account

smartRollupPublishCount.description: Number of `smart_rollup_publish` operations related to the account

smartRollupRecoverBondCount.description: Number of `smart_rollup_recover_bond` operations related to the account

smartRollupRefuteCount.description: Number of `smart_rollup_refute` operations related to the account

refutationGamesCount.description: Number of smart rollup refutation games related to the account

activeRefutationGamesCount.description: Number of active smart rollup refutation games related to the account

stakingOpsCount.description: Number of staking operations related to the account

stakingUpdatesCount.description: Number of staking updates related to the account

setDelegateParametersOpsCount.description: Number of set delegate parameters operations related to the account

dalPublishCommitmentOpsCount.description: Number of DAL publish commitment operations related to the account

firstActivity.description: Block height of the first operation, related to the account

firstActivityTime.description: Block datetime of the first operation, related to the account (ISO 8601, e.g. `2020-02-20T02:40:57Z`)

lastActivity.description: Height of the block in which the account state was changed last time

lastActivityTime.description: Datetime of the block in which the account state was changed last time (ISO 8601, e.g. `2020-02-20T02:40:57Z`)

address.description: Public key hash of the delegate (baker)

id.description: Internal TzKT id

type.description: Type of the account, `delegate` - account, registered as a delegate (baker)

address.description: Public key hash of the delegate's main key

consensusAddress.description: Public key hash of the delegate's consensus key

companionAddress.description: Public key hash of the delegate's companion key

revealed.description: Public key revelation status. Unrevealed account can't send manager operation (transaction, origination etc.)

balance.description: Total balance of the delegate (baker), including spendable and frozen funds (micro tez)

rollupBonds.description: Amount of tx rollup commitment bonds (micro tez)

smartRollupBonds.description: Amount of smart rollup commitment bonds (micro tez)

stakedBalance.description:
Amount staked from the own balance (micro tez).
Like delegated amount, except for it is frozen and can be slashed.

unstakedBalance.description: Amount that was unstaked, but not yet finalized (i.e. it is still frozen) (micro tez).

unstakedBaker.description: Information about the baker, for which there are pending unstake requests.

externalStakedBalance.description:
Amount staked from external stakers (micro tez).
Like delegated amount, except for it is frozen and can be slashed.

externalUnstakedBalance.description: Amount that was unstaked by external stakers, but not yet finalized (i.e. it is still frozen) (micro tez).

roundingError.description: Amount that was lost due to inconsistend rounding introduced in Oxford (micro tez).

totalStakedBalance.description: Total staked balance, which is `stakedBalance + externalStakedBalance`.

issuedPseudotokens.description: Total amount of issued "pseudo-tokens". These pseudotokens are used for unstaking.

frozenDepositLimit.description: Configured max amount allowed to be locked as a security deposit (micro tez)

limitOfStakingOverBaking.description: This parameter determines the maximum portion (millionth) of external stake by stakers over the baker's own staked funds.

edgeOfBakingOverStaking.description: This parameter determines the fraction (billionth) of the rewards that accrue to the baker's liquid spendable balance — the remainder accrues to frozen stakes.

counter.description: An account nonce which is used to prevent operation replay

activationLevel.description: Block height when delegate (baker) was registered as a baker last time

activationTime.description: Block datetime when delegate (baker) was registered as a baker last time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2019-11-31)

deactivationLevel.description: Block height when delegate (baker) was deactivated as a baker because of lack of funds or inactivity

deactivationTime.description: Block datetime when delegate (baker) was deactivated as a baker because of lack of funds or inactivity (ISO 8601, e.g. 2019-11-31)

ownDelegatedBalance.description: Amount delegated from the own balance (micro tez).

externalDelegatedBalance.description: Amount delegated from the external delegators (micro tez).

numContracts.description: Number of contracts, created (originated) and/or managed by the delegate (baker)

rollupsCount.description: Number of tx rollups, created (originated) by the account

smartRollupsCount.description: Number of smart rollups, created (originated) by the account

activeTokensCount.description: Number of account tokens with non-zero balances

tokenBalancesCount.description: Number of tokens the account ever had

tokenTransfersCount.description: Number of token transfers from/to the account

activeTicketsCount.description: Number of tickets the account owns.

ticketBalancesCount.description: Number of tickets the account ever owned.

ticketTransfersCount.description: Number of ticket transfers from/to the account.

numDelegators.description: Number of current delegators (accounts, delegated their funds) of the delegate (baker)

numBlocks.description: Number of baked (validated) blocks all the time by the delegate (baker)

numAttestations.description: Number of given attestations (approvals) by the delegate (baker)

numPreattestations.description: Number of given preattestations (approvals) by the delegate (baker)

numBallots.description: Number of submitted by the delegate ballots during a voting period

numProposals.description: Number of submitted (upvoted) by the delegate proposals during a proposal period

numActivations.description:
Number of account activation operations. Are used to activate accounts that were recommended allocations of
tezos tokens for donations to the Tezos Foundation’s fundraiser

numDoubleBaking.description:
Number of double baking (baking two different blocks at the same height) evidence operations,
included in blocks, baked (validated) by the delegate

numDoubleConsensus.description:
Number of double consensus (attestation or preattestation of two different blocks at the same block height) evidence operations,
included in blocks, baked (validated) by the delegate

numNonceRevelations.description: Number of seed nonce revelation (are used by the blockchain to create randomness) operations provided by the delegate

vdfRevelationsCount.description: Number of `vdf_revelation` operations included into blocks by the delegate

numRevelationPenalties.description: Number of operations for all time in which rewards were lost due to unrevealed seed nonces by the delegate (synthetic type)

numAttestationRewards.description: Number of attestation rewards received at the end of cycles (synthetic type)

numDelegations.description:
Number of all delegation related operations (new delegator, left delegator, registration as a baker),
related to the delegate (baker)

numOriginations.description: Number of all origination (deployment / contract creation) operations, related to the delegate (baker)

numTransactions.description: Number of all transaction (tez transfer) operations, related to the delegate (baker)

numReveals.description: Number of reveal (is used to reveal the public key associated with an account) operations of the delegate (baker)

numRegisterConstants.description: Number of register global constant operations sent by the baker

numSetDepositsLimits.description: Number of set deposits limit operations sent by the baker

numMigrations.description:
Number of migration (result of the context (database) migration during a protocol update) operations,
related to the delegate (synthetic type)

eq.description:
**Equal** filter mode (optional, i.e. `param.eq=123` is the same as `param=123`). \
Specify an integer number to get items where the specified field is equal to the specified value.

Example: `?balance=1234`.

ne.description:
**Not equal** filter mode. \
Specify an integer number to get items where the specified field is not equal to the specified value.

Example: `?balance.ne=1234`.

gt.description:
**Greater than** filter mode. \
Specify an integer number to get items where the specified field is greater than the specified value.

Example: `?balance.gt=1234`.

ge.description:
**Greater or equal** filter mode. \
Specify an integer number to get items where the specified field is greater than equal to the specified value.

Example: `?balance.ge=1234`.

lt.description:
**Less than** filter mode. \
Specify an integer number to get items where the specified field is less than the specified value.

Example: `?balance.lt=1234`.

le.description:
**Less or equal** filter mode. \
Specify an integer number to get items where the specified field is less than or equal to the specified value.

Example: `?balance.le=1234`.

in.description:
**In list** (any of) filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of integers to get items where the specified field is equal to one of the specified values.

Example: `?level.in=12,14,52,69`.

ni.description:
**Not in list** (none of) filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of integers to get items where the specified field is not equal to all the specified values.

Example: `?level.ni=12,14,52,69`.

eq.description:
**Equal** filter mode (optional, i.e. `param.eq=` is the same as `param=`). \
Specify an account address to get items where the specified field is equal to the specified value.

Example: `?address=tz123..`.

ne.description:
**Not equal** filter mode. \
Specify an account address to get items where the specified field is not equal to the specified value.

Example: `?address.ne=tz123..`.

in.description:
**In list** (any of) filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of account addresses to get items where the specified field is equal to one of the specified values.

Example: `?address.in=tz123..,tz345..`.

ni.description:
**Not in list** (none of) filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of account addresses to get items where the specified field is not equal to all the specified values.

Example: `?address.ni=tz123..,tz345..`.

eq.description:

Example: `?type=delegate`.

ne.description:

Example: `?type.ne=contract`.

ne.description:

Example: `?kind.ne=delegator_contract`.

in.description:

ni.description:

Example: `?kind.ni=smart_contract,asset`.

eq.description:

Specify a `tz` or `KT` address to get items where the specified field is equal to the specified value.

Example: `?sender=tz1WnfXMPaNTBmH7DBPwqCWs9cPDJdkGBTZ8`.

ne.description:

Specify a `tz` or `KT` address to get items where the specified field is not equal to the specified value.

Specify a field name to get items where the specified fields are not equal.

Example: `?sender.nex=initiator`.

eq.description:
**Equal** filter mode (optional, i.e. `param.eq=true` is the same as `param=true`). \
Specify a bool flag to get items where the specified field is equal to the specified value.

Example: `?active=true` or `?active=1` or `?active`.

null.description:
**Is null** filter mode. \
Use this mode to get items where the specified field is null or not.

Example: `?active.null` or `?active.null=false`.

fields.description:
**Fields** selection mode (optional, i.e. `select.fields=balance` is the same as `select=balance`). \
Specify a comma-separated list of fields to include into response.

Example: `?select=address,balance` => `[ { "address": "asd", "balance": 10 } ]`.

values.description:
**Values** selection mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of fields to include their values into response.

Example: `?select.values=address,balance` => `[ [ "asd", 10 ] ]`.

asc.description:
**Ascending** sort mode (optional, i.e. `sort.asc=id` is the same as `sort=id`). \
Specify a field name to sort by.

Example: `?sort=balance`.

desc.description:
**Descending** sort mode. \
Specify a field name to sort by descending.

Example: `?sort.desc=id`.

el.description:
**Elements** offset mode (optional, i.e. `offset.el=123` is the same as `offset=123`). \
Skips specified number of elements.

Example: `?offset=100`.

pg.description:
**Page** offset mode. \
Skips `page * limit` elements. This is a classic pagination.

Example: `?offset.pg=1`.

cr.description:
**Cursor** offset mode. \
Skips all elements with the `cursor` before (including) the specified value. Cursor is a field used for sorting, e.g. `id`.
Avoid using this offset mode with non-unique or non-sequential cursors such as `amount`, `balance`, etc.

Example: `?offset.cr=45837`.

payloadRound.description: Round at which the block payload was proposed

blockRound.description: Round at which the block was produced

deposit.description: Security deposit frozen on the baker's account for producing the block (micro tez)

rewardDelegated.description:
Portion of fixed reward, corresponding to delegated stake, paid to payload proposer's liquid balance (micro tez)
(it is not frozen and can be spent immediately).

rewardStakedOwn.description:
Portion of fixed reward, corresponding to baker's own stake, paid to payload proposer's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

rewardStakedEdge.description:
Portion of fixed reward, corresponding to baker's edge from external stake, paid to payload proposer's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

rewardStakedShared.description:
Portion of fixed reward, corresponding to baker's external stake, paid to payload proposer's external staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to baker's stakers).

bonusDelegated.description:
Portion of bonus reward, corresponding to delegated stake, paid to block producer's liquid balance (micro tez)
(it is not frozen and can be spent immediately).

bonusStakedOwn.description:
Portion of bonus reward, corresponding to baker's own stake, paid to block producer's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

bonusStakedEdge.description:
Portion of bonus reward, corresponding to baker's edge from external stake, paid to block producer's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

bonusStakedShared.description:
Portion of fixed reward, corresponding to baker's external stake, paid to block producer's external staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to baker's stakers).

type.description: Type of the operation, `ballot` - is used to vote for a proposal in a given voting cycle

id.description: Unique ID of the operation, stored in the TzKT indexer database

level.description: The height of the block from the genesis block, in which the operation was included

timestamp.description: Datetime of the block, in which the operation was included (ISO 8601, e.g. `2020-02-20T02:40:57Z`)

period.description: Information about the voting period for which the ballot was submitted

proposal.description: Information about the proposal for which ballot was submitted

delegate.description: Information about the delegate (baker), submitted the ballot

vote.description: Vote, given in the ballot (`yay`, `nay`, or `pass`)

quote.description: Injected historical quote at the time of operation

index.description: Voting period index, starting from zero

epoch.description: Voting epoch index, starting from zero

kind.description:
Kind of the voting period
`proposal` - delegates can submit protocol amendment proposals using the proposal operation
`exploration` -  bakers (delegates) may vote on the top-ranked proposal from the previous Proposal Period using the ballot operation
`testing` - If the proposal is approved in the Exploration Period, the testing (or 'cooldown') period begins and bakers start testing the new protocol
`promotion` - delegates can cast one vote to promote or not the tested proposal using the ballot operation
`adoption` - after the proposal is actually accepted, the ecosystem has some time to prepare to the upgrade

firstLevel.description: The height of the block in which the period starts

lastLevel.description: The height of the block in which the period ends

hash.description: Hash of the proposal, which representing a tarball of concatenated .ml/.mli source files

level.description: Height of the block from the genesis

timestamp.description: Datetime at which the block is claimed to have been created (ISO 8601, e.g. `2020-02-20T02:40:57Z`)

block.description: Block hash

baker.description: Baker expected to receive dal attestation reward

expected.description: Expected dal attestation reward, based on baker's active stake (micro tez)

rewardDelegated.description:
Reward, corresponding to delegated stake, paid to baker's liquid balance (micro tez)
(it is not frozen and can be spent immediately).

rewardStakedOwn.description:
Reward, corresponding to baker's own stake, paid to baker's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

rewardStakedEdge.description:
Reward, corresponding to baker's edge from external stake, paid to baker's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

rewardStakedShared.description:
Reward, corresponding to baker's external stake, paid to baker's external staked balance (micro tez)

type.description:
Type of the operation, `attestation` - is operation, which specifies the head of the chain as seen by the attester of a given slot.
The attester is randomly selected to be included in the block that extends the head of the chain as specified in this operation.
A block with more attestations improves the weight of the chain and increases the likelihood of that chain being the canonical one.

id.description: Unique ID of the operation, stored in the TzKT indexer database

kind.description:
Kind of the migration 
`bootstrap` - balance updates, included in the first block after genesis
`activate_delegate` - registering a new baker (delegator) during protocol migration
`airdrop` - airdrop of 1 micro tez during Babylon protocol upgrade
`proposal_invoice` - invoice for creation a proposal for protocol upgrade
`code_change` - changing contract scripts during Babylon protocol upgrade
`origination` - implicit (hardcoded in the protocol) origination of liquidity baking contracts
`subsidy` - liquidity baking subsidy
`remove_bigmap_key` - removing the key from the bigmap

account.description: Information about the account whose balance has updated as a result of the operation

balanceChange.description: The amount for which the operation updated the balance (micro tez)

diffs.description: List of bigmap updates caused by the migration.

tokenTransfersCount.description: Number of token transfers produced by the operation, or `null` if there are no transfers

path.description: Path to the bigmap in the contract storage

content.description:
Affected key.
If the action is `allocate` or `remove` the content will be `null`.

key.description: Key in JSON or Micheline format, depending on the `micheline` query parameter.

value.description:
Value in JSON or Micheline format, depending on the `micheline` query parameter.
Note, if the action is `remove_key` it will contain the last non-null value.

rewardDelegated.description:
Reward, corresponding to delegated stake, paid to baker's liquid balance (micro tez)
(it is not frozen and can be spent immediately).

rewardStakedOwn.description:
Reward, corresponding to baker's own stake, paid to baker's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

rewardStakedEdge.description:
Reward, corresponding to baker's edge from external stake, paid to baker's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

rewardStakedShared.description:
Reward, corresponding to baker's external stake, paid to baker's external staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to baker's stakers).

valueType.description: Bigmap value type as JSON schema or Micheline, depending on the `micheline` query parameter.

eq.description:
**Equal** filter mode (optional, i.e. `param.eq=123` is the same as `param=123`). \
Specify a string to get items where the specified field is equal to the specified value.

Example: `?parameters=abc`.

ne.description:
**Not equal** filter mode. \
Specify a string to get items where the specified field is not equal to the specified value.

Example: `?parameters.ne=abc`.

as.description:
**Same as** filter mode. \
Specify a string template to get items where the specified field matches the specified template. \
This mode supports wildcard `*`. Use `\*` as an escape symbol.

Example: `?parameters.as=*mid*` or `?parameters.as=*end`.

un.description:
**Unlike** filter mode. \
Specify a string template to get items where the specified field doesn't match the specified template.
This mode supports wildcard `*`. Use `\*` as an escape symbol.

Example: `?parameters.un=*mid*` or `?parameters.un=*end`.

in.description:
**In list** (any of) filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of strings to get items where the specified field is equal to one of the specified values. \
Use `\,` as an escape symbol.

Example: `?errors.in=bla,bal,abl`.

ni.description:
**Not in list** (none of) filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of strings to get items where the specified field is not equal to all the specified values. \
Use `\,` as an escape symbol.

Example: `?errors.ni=bla,bal,abl`.

null.description:
**Is null** filter mode. \
Use this mode to get items where the specified field is null or not.

Example: `?parameters.null` or `?parameters.null=false`.

eq.description:
**Equal** filter mode (optional, i.e. `param.eq=123` is the same as `param=123`). \
Specify a comma-separated list of bigmap tags to get bigmaps with exactly the same set of tags.
Avoid using this mode and use `.any` or `.all` instead, because it may not work as expected due to internal 'hidden' tags.

Example: `?tags=metadata` or `?tags=token_metadata,metadata`.

any.description:
**Has any** filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of bigmap tags to get bigmaps where at least one of the specified tags is presented.

Example: `?tags.any=metadata` or `?tags.any=token_metadata,metadata`.

all.description:
**Has all** filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of bigmap tags to get bigmaps where all of the specified tags are presented.

Example: `?tags.all=metadata` or `?tags.all=token_metadata,metadata`.

**[sortable]**

Value in JSON or Micheline format, depending on the `micheline` query parameter.
Note, if the key is inactive (removed) this field will contain the last non-null value.  
**[sortable]**

**[sortable]**

eq.description:
**Equal** filter mode (optional, i.e. `param.eq=123` is the same as `param=123`). \
Specify an expression hash to get items where the specified field is equal to the specified value.

Example: `?address=expr...`.

ne.description:
**Not equal** filter mode. \
Specify an expression hash to get items where the specified field is not equal to the specified value.

Example: `?address.ne=expr...`.

in.description:
**In list** (any of) filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of expression hashes to get items where the specified field is equal to one of the specified values.

Example: `?address.in=expr1,expr2`.

ni.description:
**Not in list** (none of) filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of expression hashes to get items where the specified field is not equal to all the specified values.

Example: `?address.ni=expr1,expr2`.

eq.description:
**Equal** filter mode (optional, i.e. `param.eq=123` is the same as `param=123`). \
Specify a JSON value to get items where the specified field is equal to the specified value.

Example: `?parameter.from=tz1...` or `?parameter.signatures.[3].[0]=null` or `?parameter.sigs.[*]=null`.

ne.description:
**Not equal** filter mode. \
Specify a JSON value to get items where the specified field is not equal to the specified value.

Example: `?parameter.ne=true` or `?parameter.amount.ne=0`.

gt.description:
**Greater than** filter mode. \
Specify a string to get items where the specified field is greater than the specified value.
Note that all stored JSON values are strings, so this will be a comparison of two strings, so we recommend comparing values of the same type,
e.g. numeric strings with numeric strings (`parameter.number.gt=123`), datetime strings with datetime strings (`parameter.date.gt=2021-01-01`), etc.
Otherwise, result may surprise you.

Example: `?parameter.balance.gt=1234` or `?parameter.time.gt=2021-02-01`.

ge.description:
**Greater or equal** filter mode. \
Specify a string to get items where the specified field is greater than equal to the specified value.
Note that all stored JSON values are strings, so this will be a comparison of two strings, so we recommend comparing values of the same type,
e.g. numeric strings with numeric strings (`parameter.number.gt=123`), datetime strings with datetime strings (`parameter.date.gt=2021-01-01`), etc.
Otherwise, result may surprise you.

Example: `?parameter.balance.ge=1234` or `?parameter.time.ge=2021-02-01`.

lt.description:
**Less than** filter mode. \
Specify a string to get items where the specified field is less than the specified value.
Note that all stored JSON values are strings, so this will be a comparison of two strings, so we recommend comparing values of the same type,
e.g. numeric strings with numeric strings (`parameter.number.gt=123`), datetime strings with datetime strings (`parameter.date.gt=2021-01-01`), etc.
Otherwise, result may surprise you.

Example: `?parameter.balance.lt=1234` or `?parameter.time.lt=2021-02-01`.

le.description:
**Less or equal** filter mode. \
Specify a string to get items where the specified field is less than or equal to the specified value.
Note that all stored JSON values are strings, so this will be a comparison of two strings, so we recommend comparing values of the same type,
e.g. numeric strings with numeric strings (`parameter.number.gt=123`), datetime strings with datetime strings (`parameter.date.gt=2021-01-01`), etc.
Otherwise, result may surprise you.

Example: `?parameter.balance.le=1234` or `?parameter.time.le=2021-02-01`.

as.description:
**Same as** filter mode. \
Specify a string template to get items where the specified field matches the specified template. \
This mode supports wildcard `*`. Use `\*` as an escape symbol.

Example: `?parameter.as=*mid*` or `?parameter.as=*end`.

un.description:
**Unlike** filter mode. \
Specify a string template to get items where the specified field doesn't match the specified template.
This mode supports wildcard `*`. Use `\*` as an escape symbol.

Example: `?parameter.un=*mid*` or `?parameter.un=*end`.

in.description:
**In list** (any of) filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of strings or JSON array to get items where the specified field is equal to one of the specified values. \

Example: `?parameter.amount.in=1,2,3` or `?parameter.in=[{"from":"tz1","to":"tz2"},{"from":"tz2","to":"tz1"}]`.

ni.description:
**Not in list** (none of) filter mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of strings to get items where the specified field is not equal to all the specified values. \
Use `\,` as an escape symbol.

Example: `?parameter.amount.ni=1,2,3` or `?parameter.ni=[{"from":"tz1","to":"tz2"},{"from":"tz2","to":"tz1"}]`.

null.description:
**Is null** filter mode. \
Use this mode to get items where the specified field is null or not.

Example: `?parameter.null` or `?parameter.null=false` or `?parameter.sigs.[0].null=false`.

fields.description:
**Fields** selection mode (optional, i.e. `select.fields=balance` is the same as `select=balance`). \
Specify a comma-separated list of fields to include into response.

Example:
`?select=address,balance as b,metadata.name as meta_name` will result in
`[ { "address": "asd", "b": 10, "meta_name": "qwe" } ]`.

values.description:
**Values** selection mode. \
Specify a comma-separated list of fields to include their values into response.

Example:
`?select.values=address,balance,metadata.name`  will result in
`[ [ "asd", 10, "qwe" ] ]`.

id.description: Internal Id, can be used for pagination

key.description: Key in JSON or Micheline format, depending on the `micheline` query parameter.

value.description:
Value in JSON or Micheline format, depending on the `micheline` query parameter.

level.description: Height of the block from the genesis

timestamp.description: Datetime at which the block is claimed to have been created (ISO 8601, e.g. `2020-02-20T02:40:57Z`)

proto.description: Protocol code, representing a number of protocol changes since genesis (mod 256, but `-1` for the genesis block)

payloadRound.description: Round at which the block payload was proposed

blockRound.description: Round at which the block was produced

deposit.description: Security deposit frozen on the baker's account for producing the block (micro tez)

rewardDelegated.description:
Portion of fixed reward, corresponding to delegated stake, paid to payload proposer's liquid balance (micro tez)
(it is not frozen and can be spent immediately).

rewardStakedOwn.description:
Portion of fixed reward, corresponding to baker's own stake, paid to payload proposer's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

rewardStakedEdge.description:
Portion of fixed reward, corresponding to baker's edge from external stake, paid to payload proposer's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

rewardStakedShared.description:
Portion of fixed reward, corresponding to baker's external stake, paid to payload proposer's external staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to baker's stakers).

bonusDelegated.description:
Portion of bonus reward, corresponding to delegated stake, paid to block producer's liquid balance (micro tez)
(it is not frozen and can be spent immediately).

bonusStakedOwn.description:
Portion of bonus reward, corresponding to baker's own stake, paid to block producer's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

bonusStakedEdge.description:
Portion of bonus reward, corresponding to baker's edge from external stake, paid to block producer's own staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to the baker).

bonusStakedShared.description:
Portion of fixed reward, corresponding to baker's external stake, paid to block producer's external staked balance (micro tez)
(it is frozen and belongs to baker's stakers).

fees.description: Total fee gathered from operations, included into the block

nonceRevealed.description:
Status of the seed nonce revelation
`true` - seed nonce revealed
`false` - there's no `seed_nonce_hash` in the block or seed nonce revelation has missed

proposer.description: Baker who proposed the block payload

lbToggle.description: Liquidity baking toggle (`true` if enabled, `false` if disabled, or `null` if the baker says 'pass')

lbToggleEma.description: Liquidity baking escape EMA value with precision of 1000000 for integer computation

attestations.description:
List of attestation (is operation, which specifies the head of the chain as seen by the attester of a given slot)
operations, included in the block

preattestations.description: List of preattestation operations, included in the block

proposals.description:
List of proposal (is used by bakers (delegates) to submit and/or upvote proposals to amend the protocol)
operations, included in the block

ballots.description: List of ballot (is used to vote for a proposal in a given voting cycle) operations, included in the block

activations.description:
List of activation (is used to activate accounts that were recommended allocations of
tezos tokens for donations to the Tezos Foundation’s fundraiser) operations, included in the block

dalEntrapmentEvidenceOps.description: List of dal entrapment evidence operations, included in the block

doubleBaking.description:
List of double baking evidence (is used by bakers to provide evidence of double baking (baking two different
blocks at the same height) by a baker) operations, included in the block

doubleConsensus.description:
List of double consensus evidence (is used by bakers to provide evidence of double (pre)attestation
((pre)attestation of two different blocks at the same block height) by a baker) operations, included in the block

nonceRevelations.description: List of nonce revelation (used by the blockchain to create randomness) operations, included in the block

vdfRevelations.description: List of vdf revelation (used by the blockchain to create randomness) operations, included in the block

delegations.description:
List of delegation (is used to delegate funds to a delegate (an implicit account registered as a baker))
operations, included in the block

originations.description: List of origination (deployment / contract creation ) operations, included in the block

transactions.description:
List of transaction (is a standard operation used to transfer tezos tokens to an account)
operations, included in the block

reveals.description: List of reveal (is used to reveal the public key associated with an account) operations, included in the block

registerConstants.description: List of register global constant operations, included in the block

setDepositsLimits.description: List of set deposits limit operations, included in the block

transferTicketOps.description: List of transfer ticket operations, included in the block

txRollupCommitOps.description: List of tx rollup commit operations, included in the block

txRollupDispatchTicketsOps.description: List of tx rollup dispatch tickets operations, included in the block

txRollupReturnBondOps.description: List of tx rollup return bond operations, included in the block

txRollupSubmitBatchOps.description: List of tx rollup submit batch operations, included in the block

increasePaidStorageOps.description: List of increase paid storage operations, included in the block

updateSecondaryKeyOps.description: List of update secondary key operations, included in the block

drainDelegateOps.description: List of drain delegate operations, included in the block

srAddMessagesOps.description: List of smart rollup add messages operations, included in the block

srCementOps.description: List of smart rollup cement operations, included in the block
